name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; covers the 7 June cutoff -> early 10 June; 8/9 June runs empty). **THE 10Y IS PINNED AT 4.52% BY A FED-HIKE REPRICING — AND THAT, NOT THE WAR, IS WHY THE METALS COMPLEX COLLAPSED AND THE WAR-RISK COMPLEX DID NOT BID. US 10Y 4.52% (-0.7% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +9.3% 3M); US 2Y 3.63%; DXY 99.91 (firm — the metals-killer).** The rates regime is the engine under everything this window: oil-fed inflation + a hot May jobs print pushed the market-implied odds of a DECEMBER Fed HIKE to ~70% (from ~45% a week ago), lifting the dollar (DXY ~99.9) and pinning the 10Y at 4.52% — which crushed the precious-metals complex (gold -2.1% to a ~23-March low, silver -24.7% 1M) and is the reason zero-yield gold could not bid a three-country missile strike on US installations. This is a DOMESTIC MACRO regime — it is TRIGGERED by the crisis (oil -> inflation) but does NOT fully reverse on Gulf de-escalation (sticky inflation + a hot labour market persist independently). India 10Y G-Sec ~7.02% (RBI held repo 5.25%). The rate stress does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: '10Y crosses 5%' STAYS watching — far below at 4.52%, not crossed. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **A HOT MAY JOBS PRINT SENT THE 10Y TO 4.54% (HIGHEST SINCE 21 MAY) AND PRICED A QUARTER-POINT HIKE BY YEAR-END — THE RATES LEG OF THE 'SELL-EVERYTHING-RATE-SENSITIVE' RISK-OFF, AND IT FIRED THE DURATION-TECH CHANNEL INTO THE AI ROUT.** May NFP printed +172K vs ~85K expected, U-rate 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m / +3.4% YoY, with Mar/Apr revised +93K — a decisively hot labour read that killed Fed-cut hopes and, per CME FedWatch, priced a quarter-point HIKE by year-end. The 10Y rose to 4.54% (+1.3% 1D, +4.1% 1M, +9.4% 3M — highest since 21 May), 2Y 3.62%, DXY 100.07 (+0.7%). This is a DOMESTIC MACRO datum — it does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. The rate move did double duty: (1) the discount-rate channel compressed equity multiples (us-10y-yield -> sp-500), and (2) the rates -> duration-tech channel fired straight into the Broadcom-led AI rout (us-10y-yield -> semiconductors, markets E1) — higher 10Y -> tech multiple compression, layered on the AI-valuation crack. THE TELL that this was a rates/AI de-risking and NOT a war risk-off: gold FELL 3.1% and oil FELL 2% even as the 10Y and DXY rose and VIX spiked ~40% — the real-yield + strong-dollar channel crushed zero-yield gold, the opposite of a flight-to-safety. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.02% (RBI held repo 5.25% — 3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias). The rate stress does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **10Y HELD ~4.49% AS OIL EASED — THE REAL-YIELD CHANNEL KEPT METALS CAPPED EVEN AS THE INFLATION IMPULSE FADED; 2Y HELD 3.62%.** With Brent softening ~1% the inflation impulse eased, but the 10Y held 4.49% (2Y 3.62%) — the structural PPI/Warsh higher-for-longer regime is sticky. The day's tell: the metals complex STABILISED (mirror image of 3 June) because the oil-down move removed the marginal inflation impulse, yet real yields at 4.49% keep zero-yield gold's opportunity cost elevated (gold a non-mover at -0.2%). TAG DISCIPLINE: the dossier cites 'Fed expected to hike by year-end (CME FedWatch)'; the 2Y holding 3.62% (below the funds-range top) is consistent with cuts-priced-out, NOT a priced hike (see fed node, 3-June fact-check). India 10Y G-Sec held ~7.02% (RBI held 5.25%, neutral). The rate stress does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **10Y ROSE TO 4.49% (+0.9%) AS MARKETS BEGAN PRICING A FED HIKE BEFORE YEAR-END — THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF THE DAY'S METALS SELLOFF.** Oil firming to ~$98 on the 3 June barrage + hardened suspension fed the energy-cost/inflation channel, and the market moved from 'no cuts' to pricing a HIKE before year-end (INN/FXEmpire: 'hawkish rate signals for this year and next'). 10Y +0.9% to 4.49%, DXY +0.3% to 99.53 — higher real yields + a stronger dollar crushed the entire metals complex (Platinum -3.5%, Palladium -3.0%, Silver -2.4%, Copper -2.3%, Gold -0.5%) on a casualty-producing crisis day, the cleanest 'inflation-event-not-risk-off' read of the cycle. The rate-path is now a STANDALONE macro driver — partly crisis-rooted (oil), but the binding non-crisis force that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.10% (1-wk high) as traders position ahead of the RBI decision this week. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **10Y TICKED UP TO ~4.46% (TO ~4.5% INTRADAY) ON THE OIL-INFLATION READ — 'HIGHER-FOR-LONGER' REINFORCED BY THE BRENT +7% GAP. The US 10Y printed ~4.46% (2Y 3.62%), reaching ~4.5% intraday on 1 June as the +7% Brent gap (Iran's suspension + Hormuz closure threat) fed the energy-cost/inflation channel and reinforced the rate-HIKE-not-cut regime. This is the same PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3 — the real-yield channel is what keeps gold from bidding (gold rose only on the SUBSEQUENT oil pullback, not safe-haven demand) and competes with EM-FX (rupee gave back to Rs95.25). Fed cuts remain fully priced OUT, post the 3.8% PCE. India 10Y G-Sec 7.03% (1 June, +3bps); next RBI reserves print ~6 June. The rate stress does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal — it's the binding non-crisis driver. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **10Y AT 4.45%, CAPPED BY A SOFTER MONTHLY PCE — FED CUTS PRICED OUT. The US 10Y held 4.45% (+0.8% MoM, +10.0% 3M); the 2Y at 3.59%. April PCE (released 28 May) printed headline 3.8% YoY (a 3-year high) but monthly +0.4% vs +0.5% expected — a softer-than-feared monthly read that capped yields and dialed back the rate-HIKE bets that had been pressuring gold. Fed cuts remain fully priced OUT (the PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3) — this does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. The softer monthly print, not the war, drove the gold rebound and the fresh US equity records.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **US 10Y 4.45% (-0.7% 1D, +2.2% MoM, +10.0% 3M) — REGIME NEAR 2026 HIGHS, BUT TODAY'S MOVE WAS CAPPED BY AN IN-LINE CORE PCE PRINT. The April PCE deflator released TODAY (May 28): headline PCE 3.8% (highest since May 2023), core PCE 3.3% (as expected, strongest since Oct). Because core came in-line — not a hot surprise — the yield reaction was muted (10Y -0.7% 1D), but the REGIME read is decisive: Fed cuts are now FULLY PRICED OUT and the market is beginning to price potential HIKES. (One source cited a 2026-high 4.67% intraday; the in-line core print likely capped the move — note as caveat.) The real-yield channel is the day's transmission engine: elevated real yields (oil↑→inflation↑→real-yields↑) pinned gold BELOW $4,400 to a 2-month low even on a live US-Iran ballistic-missile-exchange day — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest decisively. US 2Y 3.59%. This is the PPI/Warsh regime doing double duty: capping gold and competing with EM-FX, structural through Q3 2026 and NOT reversible on a Hormuz reopening.** PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **US 10Y 4.48% (~-0.2% 1D, +3.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M) — ELEVATED, AND IT IS THE CYCLE'S CLEANEST NON-CRISIS DRIVER. At 4.48% the 10Y is doing two things at once: (1) CAPPING GOLD — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest, which is the proximate reason gold did NOT bid the May 25 strikes (see gold node C5); (2) COMPETING WITH EM-FX — elevated US real yields are a structural rupee headwind independent of oil (a steady FII/portfolio-outflow pull). The inflation-via-oil pass-through is STICKY: yields are +13.1% over 3M even as Brent de-prices ~-14% MoM, which says the bond market has banked the inflation pulse and does not expect it to fully reverse. The rate stress does NOT unwind on a Hormuz reopening — it is the PPI/Warsh regime, structural through Q3 2026. US 2Y 3.58% (curve still steep). VIX 16.7 cycle-low = no escalation priced. Next regime data: Friday May 30 PCE deflator (Fed's preferred gauge).** PRIOR (May 24): Day 86 (Sunday May 24 — markets closed; US Memorial Day Monday May 25). **US 10Y 4.56% FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+5.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M). US 2Y 3.59% — 2s/10s = +97bps (steep). THE BOND MARKET IS NOT SPIKING ON OIL-INFLATION FEARS DESPITE OIL AT $103.54 — WELL ABOVE $73.80 PRE-CRISIS. NORMALLY A $30 OIL MOVE WOULD LIFT 10Y YIELDS 20-30BPS BUT 10Y IS UP ONLY +5.4% MoM. THE BOND MARKET IS PRICING THE INFLATION PULSE AS TRANSITORY — DEAL EXPECTATION EMBEDDED. IF DEAL FAILS, 10Y COULD SPIKE 20-40BPS QUICKLY AND THAT'S ITS OWN EQUITY HEADWIND. NEXT US DATA: TUESDAY MAY 26 CONFERENCE BOARD CONSUMER CONFIDENCE + CASE-SHILLER; FRIDAY MAY 30 PCE DEFLATOR (FED'S PREFERRED INFLATION GAUGE).** PCE Friday is the next regime-relevant print. **Reversibility**: deal-failure case re-prices 10Y +20-40bps within 5-10 sessions; durable PPI-Warsh regime keeps 10Y in 4.50-4.65% band. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.09%** flat into Monday Indian-only session; RBI Tuesday May 26 $5B 3Y swap auction is the institutional inflection. PRIOR (May 23): US 10Y 4.56% MAY 22 CLOSE — WARSH REGIME PERSISTENCE AT HIGHER LEVEL; APRIL MINUTES (WED MAY 20) SHOWED HISTORIC DISSENT COUNT. US 2Y 3.59% (+0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, -0.1% 3M) — FLAT, FED PRICING AT NEUTRAL HOLD.** **Primary chain (~60% inflation regime intact)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY structural through Q3 2026; Warsh effective May 16 — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Fed April minutes May 20 'important event for new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh' (Kiplinger) — historic dissent count from FOMC hold; bond market read: hawkish dissent supports steeper curve. **Secondary chain (~25% deal-anticipation)**: Brent flat-to-down 1M (-1.5%) + WTI broke $100 to $96.60 → oil pass-through to PPI compression (modest, not durable); deal-anticipation tape removed marginal inflation-cascade overhang. **Tertiary chain (~15% Nvidia earnings risk-on)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-cycle bid pulled risk-on flows into equity, allowing 10Y to ease modestly. **Cross-asset confirmation**: VIX 16.70 cycle low + S&P 7,473.47 +0.4% + 10Y eased + Gold $4,521 underperforming on real-yield channel + deal-anticipation amplification. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00%** (paused near 7.00% after 4-session advance per Trading Economics May 22; -0.03 to -0.07pp est. WoW) — eased on RBI swap-auction announcement + government bond-tax-cut study. **DXY 99.32** +0.1% 1D / +0.5% 1M / +1.7% 3M — broadly stable. **Reversibility**: Crisis component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (10Y bounces back); inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026; Warsh transition (~15%) takes ~90 days to clarify. **Next data**: May PPI/CPI cycle (June 13 schedule per BLS) — until then yields oscillate ~4.50-4.65%. **Decision-relevant**: 10Y easing into Nvidia print is NOT a peace-dividend signal; it's a temporary risk-on flow. The PPI-Warsh regime is the binding force. PRIOR (May 22): **US 10Y 4.56% -0.5% 1D / +5.5% 1M / +13.2% 3M — EASING INTO NVIDIA-EARNINGS RISK-ON; PPI-WARSH REGIME INTACT AT HIGHER LEVEL. US 2Y 3.58% 0.0% 1D / -0.4% 1M / -0.2% 3M.** **Primary chain (~60% inflation regime intact)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY structural through Q3 2026; Warsh effective May 16 — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. **Secondary chain (~25% deal-anticipation)**: Brent crash -5.3% across window + WTI broke $100 to $99.50 → oil pass-through to PPI compression (modest, not durable); deal-anticipation tape removed a marginal inflation-cascade overhang. **Tertiary chain (~15% Nvidia earnings risk-on)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-cycle bid pulled risk-on flows into equity, allowing 10Y to ease modestly. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + Nvidia-earnings risk-on**: VIX 16.61 (cycle low) + S&P/NASDAQ +0.6%/+0.4% + 10Y -0.5% + Gold -0.5% (gold still underperforming on real-yield channel + deal-anticipation amplification). **India 10Y G-Sec 7.07% -0.03pp 1D** eased on RBI swap-auction announcement + government bond-tax-cut study; foreign-investor channel partial relief. **DXY 99.23 0.0% 1D / +0.4% 1M / +1.6% 3M** broadly stable; DXY-INR decoupling on RBI defense. **Reversibility**: Crisis component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (10Y bounces back); inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026 at minimum; Warsh transition (~15%) takes ~90 days to clarify. **Next regime data**: May PPI/CPI cycle (June 13 / June 13 schedule per BLS) — until then, yields oscillate ~4.50-4.65%. **Decision-relevant**: 10Y easing into Nvidia print is NOT a peace-dividend signal; it's a temporary risk-on flow. The PPI-Warsh regime is the binding force. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **US 10Y 4.59% (carries forward Friday close, +3.0% cumulative Fri+Mon, +6.6% 1M, +13.4% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% flat; US 30Y ~5.12% (flat-to-up vs Friday, 10-month high)** per markets dossier Section A. Bloomberg 'worst weekly Treasury rout in a year' framing carries from Friday. **Primary chain (~70% rates regime)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m (BLS May 13, biggest since Dec 2022) + Warsh effective May 16 (rate-hike-before-year-end now in CNBC/Yahoo discussion) — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027; rate-cut bets now sit beyond 2026. **Secondary chain (~30% crisis)**: Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday (StanChart $130-145 path activates same day) → oil pass-through to PPI intensifies → producer-side inflation feed-through to Treasury yields. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.05-7.10% flat** (+6-8 bp 1M, +13 bp 3M); investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield ~7.05%; FII debt outflows continuing. **Cross-asset stagflation regime intact**: 10Y +14 bp Friday + DXY 99.22 firm + Gold -0.4% (real-yield channel competing) + VIX 18.43 sub-20 + S&P -1.24% Friday. **Tuesday May 19 NSC binary**: strike orders → yields gap higher again (energy pass-through intensifies); back-off → yields hold or modestly retrace; nothing operational → yields oscillate. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE YIELDS ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: US 10Y 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y 5.121% (highest since May 2025).** Per markets dossier Section A: India 10Y G-Sec 7.09% (+5 bps 1D May 15) — rebound from prior decline; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **STAGFLATION REGIME UNCHANGED**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY closed Fed easing path indefinitely; Warsh effective May 16. **Monday May 18 open is the test** — if NYT preparation report is fully priced into Brent and oil gaps higher, yields move higher again; if markets discount the report as posturing, yields hold. **Tertiary chain (~15% Iran) intensifies**: Trump 2-month ultimatum letter + NYT preparation report adds tail-risk premium. Reversibility unchanged: crisis component (~15%) reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; PPI/CPI inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026 at minimum; Warsh transition (~25%) takes ~90 days to clarify; risk asymmetric to hawkish surprise. **PRIOR (May 16):** STAGFLATION BOND ROUT — US 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (highest since May 2025) — Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.'** Primary chain (~60% inflation): **April PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13)** confirms producer-side inflation has fully absorbed the energy shock — Core PPI +1% (vs 0.4% est), ~60% of rise was in services, energy at the root; combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12) Fed easing path is closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027 earlier in the week; CNBC/Yahoo reporting markets now consider rate hike possible. Secondary chain (~25% Fed transition): **Warsh confirmed 54-45 May 13 replacing Powell effective today May 16**; Warsh's preference for trimmed mean inflation measures = sees underlying inflation cooler than headline, but Warsh shrinks balance sheet + relies on rate tool = potentially more hawkish on QE channel; markets uncertain about reaction function for first 90 days = term premium expanding. Tertiary chain (~15% Iran): Trump 2-month ultimatum letter creates a defined uncertainty window; oil pass-through to PPI documented and ongoing. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (flat-to-easing)**: traders sidelined ahead of Trump-Xi summit; RBI bond-tax cut for foreigners under consideration; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **Cross-asset stagflation confirmation**: 10Y +14bp / 30Y +11bp / DXY +0.4% / Silver -9.1% / Gold -2.6% / S&P -1.24% / VIX 17.26→18.43 / WTI +4.2%. The paper-equity leg of the cycle-extreme bifurcation reversed within 24 hours toward crisis confirmation. PRIOR (May 15): **US 10Y 4.46% MAY 15 (-0.4% 1D, +4.2% 1M, +10.1% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% (-0.3% 1D, -0.7% 1M, -0.1% 3M).** Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **April CPI 3.8% YoY + April PPI +1.4% m/m closed near-term Fed easing door**; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Real-yield channel keeps gold in regime-shift unwind (gold -2.2% 1D); DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D) — modest dollar bid on Trump-Xi joint statement. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + paper-physical bifurcation at extreme**: VIX 17.26 cycle low + S&P/NASDAQ/Dow record closes + 10Y at 4.46% holding all reinforce paper-side complacency on a day with two new kinetic incidents and an Israeli KIA. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (DealPlexus / CCIL May 14 — yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior). **US 10Y 4.48% MAY 14 (+0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M, +10.5% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, +0.2% 3M).** CPI absorption — Fed easing closed (April CPI 3.8% three-year high May 12; energy +17.9% YoY contributed >40% headline gain; core +2.8% YoY). Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **Real-yield channel keeps gold underperforming** ($4,701.50 +0.1% 1D — modestly underperforming bear case on Trump-Xi opening). India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign-cut absorbed; +bps vs recent 6.96% range). DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D, +0.4% 1M, +1.4% 3M) — dollar failing to bid on crisis day (G5). **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime: VIX 17.87 sub-18 + S&P 500 +0.6% + 10Y at 4.48% holding all reinforce that markets see crisis as INFLATION + FLOW event** (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT financial-system stress event. PRIOR (May 9): **US 10Y 4.36% MAY 8 CLOSE (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M, +4.0% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, +0.2% 1M).** Modest 10Y pullback on stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print (per Reuters/Investing 'labor market resilience') reduced immediate June-cut probability; longer-end yields easing back from FOMC-driven highs. **Real-yield channel still keeping gold underperforming:** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold $4,720.40 (+0.4% 1D) underperformed on a crisis-day with May 7 destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime:** VIX cycle-low 17.08 + S&P 500 record close 7,398.93 + 10Y not making new highs all reinforce that markets see the West Asia crisis as an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT a financial-system stress event (which would be positive for gold + bid for 10Y). Cheniere $3.5B Q1 GAAP loss on derivative MtM is the closest thing to a financial-stress signal but is firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0% (+6-8 bps cycle, ~+15bps from late-Apr lows, ~+25bps from pre-crisis) — rupee weakness + oil inflation + FII channel keeping pressure. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M) modest dollar weakness. Powell verbal 'hasn't even peaked yet' on energy inflation still operative; CME FedWatch June-cut probability now low.

current.delta_1d: 2Y +0.8% / 10Y ~flat -- hawkish-Fed front-end repricing (curve flattening); DXY broke >100.
current.driver: THE STICKY CHANNEL. Oil -4% on deal hopes but the 10Y held 4.49% -- May CPI 4.2% and the December-hike repricing are domestic and do not un-print on a Hormuz deal. This is why US equities only rose +0.5% while oil-importers (India +2-3%, Europe +1.8%) rallied hard. India 10Y eases on the oil-down (importer benefit).

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: the Fed-path -> yield channel is the engine under the window — oil-fed inflation + hot May jobs pushed market-implied December Fed-HIKE odds to ~70% (from ~45% a week ago), pinning the 10Y at 4.52% and lifting DXY to ~99.9, which crushed metals (gold -2.1%) and explains why the war-risk complex did not bid. Weight stays 10 (formula caps; at ceiling).

2026-06-06: the discount-rate channel fired — the hot May jobs print pushed the 10Y to 4.54% (highest since 21 May), compressing equity multiples and co-driving the broad 5-June risk-off (S&P -2.6%, NASDAQ -4.2%) alongside the Broadcom AI crack. Weight stays 10 (formula caps; already at ceiling).

2026-06-06 (markets E1): ADDED. The rates -> duration-tech channel fired hard — the hot May jobs print took the 10Y to 4.54%, compressing long-duration tech multiples directly into the Broadcom-led AI rout (SOX largest 1D plunge since March 2020). STRENGTHEN per the edge proposal; first explicit activation (act 1). Initial weight 5.0 for a 2nd-order systemic channel that genuinely fired (directness 1.5; not inflated on one session).

2026-05-27 (markets dossier C5): US 10Y at 4.48% is the proximate cap on gold — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest against elevated real yields, which (with a firm DXY) is why gold did NOT bid the May 25 strikes (-0.4%). This is the inverse-relationship channel that defines whether the crisis bids gold or the dollar. First explicit activation as a distinct yield→gold edge (the gold↔fed channel already carries the rate-expectations leg).

recent_signals.headline: Hawkish-Fed signature: US 2Y +0.8% to 3.65% (front-end prices the hike), 10Y ~flat at 4.46% (flattening), DXY +0.8% to >100. The rates channel is stickier, not looser -- caps equity upside and pressures EM-FX. CONFIRMED.

US 10Y eased to 4.43% on oil-down disinflation + pre-FOMC positioning; the FOMC decision (17 Jun) is the near-term swing factor for whether the rates channel loosens. India 10Y 6.863% (oil-down + FAR/tax-relief inflows). CONFIRMED.

US 10Y eased only -0.4% to 4.47% despite oil -5% — the de-escalation does NOT reverse the May CPI print or the Fed's hawkish hold (FOMC this week); the US rates channel remains sticky. India 10Y ~6.85-6.91%, easing on oil-down + FAR/tax-relief FPI debt inflows (Rs11,026cr/4d). CONFIRMED.

10Y ~4.49% — BARELY MOVED even as oil fell 4%: the 'deal helps oil-importers, not the Fed' split. De-escalation eases oil but does NOT un-print May CPI 4.2% or reverse the December-hike repricing -- the US's binding constraint is domestic and sticky. DXY soft (99.81) on risk-on. India 10Y eased to ~6.85-6.90% on the oil-down + G-sec tax exemption.

10Y ~4.53% -- BARELY MOVED on the 4.2% CPI print: the bond market had PRE-PRICED the inflation/hike path; equities had not (hence the -1.6% S&P vs a pinned 10Y). DXY 100.26 (+0.3%, back above 100). India 10Y G-Sec fell to ~6.90% (4-week low) on the G-sec FII tax-exemption ordinance -- a clean policy win on the DEBT channel despite oil up and the US CPI shock abroad.

**THE 10Y PINNED AT 4.52% BY A FED-HIKE REPRICING — THE ENGINE UNDER THE METALS COLLAPSE AND THE NON-BID OF THE WAR-RISK COMPLEX. 10Y 4.52% (-0.7% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +9.3% 3M); 2Y 3.63%; DXY 99.91 (firm).** Oil-fed inflation + a hot May jobs print pushed market-implied DECEMBER Fed-HIKE odds to ~70% (from ~45% a week ago), lifting the dollar (~99.9) and pinning the 10Y at 4.52%. The elevated real yield + firm dollar CRUSHED the precious-metals complex (gold -2.1% to a ~23-March low, silver -24.7% 1M) and is the proximate reason zero-yield gold could not bid a three-country missile strike on US installations. This is a DOMESTIC MACRO regime — TRIGGERED by the crisis (oil -> inflation) but it does NOT fully reverse on Gulf de-escalation (sticky inflation + a hot labour market persist independently); critical for deployment timing. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.02% (RBI held repo 5.25%). The rate stress does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. '10Y crosses 5%' trigger stays watching, far below.
sources: Fed-path: market-implied December Fed-HIKE odds ~70% (from ~45% a week ago) on hot May jobs + oil-fed inflation (CME FedWatch / market-implied)
Cross-asset: gold -2.1% / silver -24.7% 1M as real yields + DXY firm; war-risk complex (gold, Brent, VIX) did not bid the maximum kinetic escalation

recent_signals.headline: **HOT MAY JOBS SENT THE 10Y TO 4.54% (HIGHEST SINCE 21 MAY) AND PRICED A QUARTER-POINT HIKE BY YEAR-END — THE RATES LEG OF THE RISK-OFF, FIRING THE DURATION-TECH CHANNEL INTO THE AI ROUT.** May NFP +172K vs ~85K exp, U-rate 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m / +3.4% YoY, Mar/Apr revised +93K — a decisively hot read that killed Fed-cut hopes and, per CME FedWatch, priced a quarter-point HIKE by year-end. 10Y 4.54% (+1.3% 1D, +4.1% 1M, +9.4% 3M), 2Y 3.62%, DXY 100.07 (+0.7%). A DOMESTIC MACRO datum — does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. Double duty: discount-rate channel compressed equity multiples (-> sp-500); rates -> duration-tech channel fired into the Broadcom-led AI rout (-> semiconductors). THE TELL: gold FELL 3.1% and oil FELL 2% as 10Y + DXY rose and VIX spiked ~40% — a rates/AI de-risking, not a war risk-off (real yields + strong dollar crushed zero-yield gold). India 10Y ~7.02% (RBI held repo 5.25%, 3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias). Does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal.

April PCE (28 May) printed headline 3.8% YoY (3-yr high) but monthly +0.4% vs +0.5% expected — a softer read that capped yields and dialed back the rate-HIKE bets pressuring gold. Cuts remain fully priced out (PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3); does not reverse on a Hormuz deal.

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.45% (-0.7% 1D, +2.2% MoM, +10.0% 3M) — REGIME NEAR 2026 HIGHS, BUT TODAY'S MOVE CAPPED BY AN IN-LINE CORE PCE; FED CUTS FULLY PRICED OUT, HIKES BEING PRICED.** (1) **April PCE released TODAY (May 28)**: headline PCE 3.8% (highest since May 2023), core PCE 3.3% (as expected, strongest since Oct). Because core printed IN-LINE — not a hot surprise — the yield reaction was muted (10Y -0.7% 1D); but the regime read is decisive: the Fed easing path is closed indefinitely and the market is now beginning to price potential HIKES rather than cuts. (2) **The 2026-high caveat**: one source cited an intraday 2026-high of 4.67%; the in-line core print likely capped the move back to 4.45% — flag as a data caveat, not a confirmed close. (3) **Real-yield channel is the day's transmission engine**: elevated real yields (oil↑→inflation↑→real-yields↑) pinned gold BELOW $4,400 to a 2-month low even on a day with a LIVE US-Iran ballistic-missile exchange (IRGC fired one missile at a US base in Kuwait, intercepted, zero casualty — calibrated). Zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest decisively; the safe-haven flow did NOT go to gold. (4) US 2Y 3.59% (curve steep). **This is the PPI/Warsh regime doing double duty — capping gold and competing with EM-FX — structural through Q3 2026 and NOT reversible on a Hormuz reopening.** India 10Y G-Sec ~7.03% STALE (last live May 25 — needs CCIL refresh).

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.48% (~-0.2% 1D, +3.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M) — ELEVATED; THE CLEANEST NON-CRISIS DRIVER, DOING DOUBLE DUTY: CAPPING GOLD AND COMPETING WITH EM-FX.** (1) **Real-yield suppression of gold**: at 4.48% the 10Y is the proximate reason gold did NOT bid the May 25 US strikes / Lebanon ground op — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest, and the safe-haven flow went into the DOLLAR (DXY 99.22) instead (see gold node C5). (2) **EM-FX competition**: elevated US real yields are a structural rupee headwind INDEPENDENT of oil — a steady portfolio-outflow pull that helps explain why India refused the oil-relief bounce (rupee back to ₹95.68). (3) **Sticky pass-through**: yields are +13.1% over 3M even as Brent de-prices ~-14% MoM — the bond market has banked the inflation-via-oil pulse and does not expect it to fully reverse; the PPI/Warsh regime is structural through Q3 2026 and does NOT unwind on a Hormuz reopening. (4) US 2Y 3.58% (curve steep); VIX 16.7 cycle-low = no escalation priced. **Next regime data: Friday May 30 PCE deflator (Fed's preferred inflation gauge).** India 10Y G-Sec ~7.03% STALE (last live May 25 — needs CCIL refresh).
